The goal bearing is +148°, which is to the right of the current heading (+90°). The difference is 58°.
The distance has been consistently increasing with every MOVE_FORWARD action (8001 -> ... -> 15136). This clearly indicates that moving forward in the current direction is causing significant regression.
The bearing is also increasing (93 -> ... -> 148), suggesting the goal is getting relatively further to the right as we move forward. This implies we are moving in a direction that is not aligned with the goal.
The past experience explicitly states: "When behind_left & far: MOVE_FORWARD → regress... Wrong: moving forward when bearing is behind_left increases distance. Turn first."
Although the bearing is +148° (which is technically "behind_right" if we consider 0° as forward, 90° as right, 180° as behind, 270° as left), the pattern of regression is identical to the "behind_left" case described in the experience. The key takeaway is that moving forward when the goal is not directly ahead (or slightly off) causes regression.
The goal is to the right (+14
